narrative:
The 2026 midterm election season begins Tuesday with primary elections in Texas, Arkansas, and North Carolina, featuring what has become the most expensive Senate primary on record [1][3][5]. The Texas Senate race has captured national attention as both parties face competitive primaries that could determine control of a seat Democrats haven't won statewide since 1994 [1][2][5].

## Republican Primary: Three-Way Battle

Incumbent Sen. John Cornyn faces a challenging primary against Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton and Rep. Wesley Hunt in what sources describe as a race marked by "nothing short of vitriol" [1]. Cornyn, endorsed by the National Republican Senatorial Committee and seeking his fifth term, has attacked Paxton over his "alleged infidelity, his pending divorce and his 2023 impeachment trial" [1]. A pro-Cornyn PAC has accused Paxton of being a "wife-cheater" and a "fraud" [1].

Paxton has responded by accusing Cornyn of running a "kamikaze campaign" that will ultimately help Democrats [1]. Despite the attacks, most polls show Paxton as the frontrunner, with his track record of opposing President Biden and backing President Trump making him "a favorite of the MAGA crowd" [3]. Hunt, 44, elected to Congress in 2022, has positioned himself as a political newcomer and "more conservative choice than Cornyn" [3].

Notably, President Trump has not endorsed any candidate in the race, though he had "complimentary words for both Cornyn and Paxton" during a recent Texas visit [3]. All three candidates emphasize their alignment with Trump, with Cornyn claiming he "voted with Trump 99% of the time" [1].

## Democratic Primary: Style and Strategy Divide

The Democratic primary features State Rep. James Talarico and Rep. Jasmine Crockett in what sources describe as an "increasingly nasty campaign" [3]. The candidates represent different approaches to Democratic politics in the Trump era [5].

Talarico, 36, a "former middle school teacher and current seminary student," has campaigned on a "politics of love" message, arguing the central divide is "not left v right" but "top v bottom" [5]. He frequently quotes "Love thy neighbor" and believes "the key to getting things done is bridging the gap between two parties" [2].

Crockett, 44, a civil rights lawyer, is described as "a firebrand progressive whose unsparing attacks on Trump and Republicans have earned her a starring role in the resistance movement" [5]. She has a "more fiery approach, known for her intense language and sparring with Republicans" [2] and argues that "high turnout among young voters and voters of color – not ideological moderation – is the key to winning statewide" [5].

## Election Mechanics and Broader Context

Texas law requires candidates to receive at least 50% of the vote to win outright, with a runoff scheduled for May 26 if necessary [4]. There is "a very good likelihood that with three candidates on the Republican side that no one does get 51% of that vote" [2].

The races have generated unprecedented spending, with "over $122 million dedicated to ad spending and reservations" making this the "most expensive Senate primary on record" [1][5]. Early voting has "soared, particularly on the Democratic side" [5].

Polls close at varying times: North Carolina at 7:30 p.m. EST, Texas at 8 p.m. EST, and Arkansas at 8:30 p.m. EST [4]. The outcomes will provide early indicators for the broader 2026 midterm elections, where "Democrats are favored to win the House and Republicans seem likely to keep the Senate" [3].
